title: schoenus squamellatus
intro: Schoenus squamellatus, also known as the swamp sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, offering potential health benefits for immune system support and relief from inflammation. In herbal preparations, Schoenus squamellatus is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its essential oil can also be extracted for therapeutic use.
